This family includes a wide variety of flowering plants, commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. The genus Ligularia itself is a group of flowering plants native to Asia, known for their large, showy flowers and coarse foliage. The classification of Ligularia melanocephala reflects its unique characteristics and its relationship to other plants within the Asteraceae family.

intro: Ligularia melanocephala, also known as the leopard plant, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family, native to China and Japan. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and aiding in digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health issues. The bioactive constituents of Ligularia melanocephala include sesquiterpenoids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
